sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 21.26 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.21)

statement.latex: (F.\,Lisi, L.\,Sabatini). Let$G$be a non-trivial finite group and let$p_1, , p_k$be the distinct prime divisors of$|G|$. For each$i$, let$H_i$be a Sylow$p_i$-subgroup of$G$. Is it true that there exists an element$x G$such that for all$i$the subgroup$H_i H_i^x$is inclusion-minimal in$\{H_i H_i^g \,: \, g G\}$? { T.\,C.\,Burness}

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
